evals.prompt: I want to be able to jump back to recently-edited files in nvim without leaving home row. Currently I'm doing :e with tab-completion which is annoying.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs scan_tools, notices fzf exists in tools/ but isn't wired into nvim, suggests at least telescope.nvim, harpoon, and an fzf-lua integration. Groups by type (existing/addon/new). Asks which to try before installing anything.

evals.prompt: tmux feels really basic. I want to feel like my tmux is a cockpit — session restoration, easier pane resizing, maybe a status bar with git info.
evals.expected_output: Multiple suggestions covering tpm + tmux-resurrect + tmux-continuum, custom status bar mods to existing tmux.conf.j2, and possibly a brand-new tool suggestion like zellij for comparison. Install plan for tpm uses git_clone not brew. On approval, promote writes a proper templated config that matches repo conventions.

evals.prompt: give me a nicer way to browse my clipboard history on mac
evals.expected_output: Suggestions include maccy, clipboard-manager alternatives, and notes whether any existing tool covers this. Install via brew cask if GUI. On reject, uninstall cleanly removes it via brew. Promote writes a darwin-only playbook (no debian/arch since it's mac-specific).
